preamble: The reduction of the voiced alveolar fricative [z] into a neutral schwa [ə] or its complete elision is a documented form of lenition, typically occurring in unstressed grammatical markers or as a result of vowel-consonant mergers in connected speech.

examples.note: Widespread weakening of unstressed auxiliary sibilants where the fricative is reduced to a vocalic off-glide or disappears entirely.
Historical reduction of final segments in unstressed syllables where dental fricatives were replaced by reduced central vowels before complete loss.

sources: Phonological research on English grammatical marker elision.
